description_long: This bungalow features a prominent, steeply pitched gable roof constructed from wooden shingles, with a cross-gabled section extending over a covered porch area. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, complemented by dark timber framing around the windows and corners. The front facade includes a small porch with stone steps, potted plants, and a blue-tiled water feature, creating a welcoming entrance. Multiple rectangular windows are symmetrically arranged along the sides, allowing ample natural light into the interior. The base of the structure is surrounded by a strip of green grass and a low brick foundation, enhancing its integration with a suburban or park-like setting. The overall design is compact and inviting, with natural materials and earthy tones dominating the visual palette.
description: A compact wooden bungalow with a steep gable roof, stone porch, and symmetrical windows, set on a grassy base with a welcoming entrance and natural materials.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long expanse of the wooden gable roof, three symmetrically placed windows, and a hedge along the base.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the cross-gabled roof, front porch with stone steps, blue water feature, and potted plants.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the main entrance, stone porch, water feature, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back gable, upper window, and additional side windows with a hedge at the base.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with grouped side windows
